general_info.definition: Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) used as precursor material for synthesizing honeycomb hard carbon anodes for potassium-ion batteries.

method.conditions: Stirring for 1h, then standing in ambient environment for 72h

post_treatment: Ultrasonic dispersion in HCl (1 mol L-1) to remove redundant NaOH
Centrifugation and washing with deionized water until neutral pH

applications.performance: Derived honeycomb hard carbon (HHC) anode exhibits 195.3 mAh g⁻¹ at 0.1 A g⁻¹ with 67.43% capacity retention after 150 cycles
applications.comparative_advantage: HHC derived from CQDs shows superior cycling stability

feedback.label: Bad
feedback.expert_comment: Omission Error: The field 'carbon_dots -> properties -> structural -> size -> distribution' contained 'diameters 3.0-5.0 nm' in the source text, but it was extracted as null/missing.
